data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences $158 Million Liquidation in 24 Hours
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market saw liquidations totaling $158 million over the past 24 hours. Long positions accounted for $73.45 million of the liquidations, while short positions amounted to $84.48 million. This significant activity highlights the volatility and risk inherent in the cryptocurrency trading environment.

data.title: "If I Lose, DeFi Dies”: Tornado Cash Founder Sounds Alarm Ahead of Trial
data.description:
The founder of Tornado Cash, a notable figure in the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector, has issued a dire warning ahead of their upcoming trial, stating, "If I lose this case, it could have catastrophic consequences for the entire DeFi ecosystem." This statement reflects a deep concern for the potential repercussions that the legal proceedings may have on the future of decentralized finance and the innovative technologies that underpin it.
source: https://voiceofcrypto.online/news/tornado-cash-founder-defi-dies-doj

data.title: Global Markets React to Escalating Middle East Tensions
data.description:
According to PANews, tensions in the Middle East escalated unexpectedly on Friday, significantly impacting global commodities. Crude oil prices surged by approximately 6%, marking a weekly increase of over 10%. Meanwhile, spot gold prices rose sharply by nearly $50, firmly surpassing the $3,400 mark, with a weekly gain of more than 3.7%.

In the coming week, several central banks, including those of the United States, the United Kingdom, and Japan, are scheduled to hold policy meetings. The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is expected to remain a focal point.

Key events to watch in the market include the release of the U.S. June New York Fed Manufacturing Index on Monday at 20:30 UTC+8, and the Bank of Japan's interest rate decision on Tuesday. On Wednesday at 20:30 UTC+8, the U.S. will release initial jobless claims data for the week ending June 14.

On Thursday, the Federal Reserve's Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) will announce its interest rate decision and economic projections at 02:00 UTC+8, followed by a monetary policy press conference by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell at 02:30 UTC+8.

Next Tuesday, the U.S. monthly retail sales data will be closely monitored. Investors are eager to determine whether tariffs have led to price increases, potentially affecting consumer spending. With the 90-day pause on U.S. President Donald Trump's series of tariff measures set to end on July 8, trade developments are likely to continue causing market uncertainty.

data.title: Dormant Ethereum ICO Address Activated After Nearly a Decade
data.description: According to BlockBeats, an Ethereum ICO address that had been inactive for 9.9 years was recently activated. The address contained 2,000 ETH, currently valued at approximately $5,063,918. In 2015, these ETH were worth only about $620. The address executed two transactions, each transferring 0.001 ETH.

data.title: A certain address withdrew ZKJ and KOGE liquidity and then sold them in large amounts, which may cause the two coins to fall by 3% in a short period of time
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, ZKJ and KOGE, which have always been stable, fell by 3% in a short period of time between 21:20 and 22:05 today, which is suspected to be caused by the withdrawal of liquidity and continuous selling of address 0x364...f18e9.
The address withdrew 1.29 million ZKJ and 8667 KOGE bilateral liquidity from OKX at 21:49, and then:
A total of about 3.1 million US dollars of ZKJ were sold at 21:21 and 22:05;
About 550,000 US dollars of KOGE were sold at 21:20.
At 21:21, ZKJ's trading volume surged to 12.73 million US dollars, triggering a continuous decline within 15 minutes, which may have caused a chain reaction due to LP panic withdrawal; a similar situation occurred at 22:05. KOGE was also frustrated at the same time because it was closely tied to ZKJ, although the amount of selling was not large.

data.title: Ethereum ICO whale address that has been dormant for 9.9 years has been activated
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to on-chain data, a wallet address that has not been active since the Ethereum ICO in 2015 was activated today, and it has been dormant for about 9.9 years. The address currently still holds 2,000 ETH, which is valued at about $5.06 million at the current price, and was only worth about $620 that year.
The address only transferred 0.001 ETH in two transactions this time, and the specific purpose is not yet clear.

data.title: Bitcoin must upgrade or fall victim to quantum computing in 5 years
data.description:
Unless Bitcoin upgrades its core cryptography in the next five years, the trust it has built over 16 years could be wiped out by a single quantum attack. Urgent upgrades are needed to protect the world’s leading cryptocurrency.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-quantum-computing?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Singapore Police and StraitsX Collaborate to Combat Cryptocurrency Money Laundering
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the Singapore Police Force, in collaboration with cryptocurrency platform StraitsX, conducted a joint enforcement operation from May 13 to 30 to tackle money laundering activities involving cryptocurrencies. During the operation, authorities seized over 200,000 Singapore dollars in cash, and 35 men and 14 women, aged between 18 and 58, are currently assisting with investigations.

Preliminary investigations suggest that these individuals allegedly allowed others to use their cryptocurrency or Singpass accounts in exchange for payments ranging from 400 to 3,000 Singapore dollars. These accounts are suspected of being used for money laundering purposes. The police noted that most suspects were contacted by unknown individuals through Telegram or WhatsApp and provided their personal information as instructed.

The police emphasized that partnering with StraitsX, a registered digital payment service provider, has been instrumental in utilizing technological solutions to detect suspicious accounts and take swift action. Under Singapore's Corruption, Drug Trafficking and Other Serious Crimes Act, assisting others in retaining criminal proceeds can result in a maximum penalty of three years' imprisonment, a fine of 50,000 Singapore dollars, or both. The police urged the public to refrain from lending their bank or cryptocurrency accounts to avoid involvement in criminal activities.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America and TD Bank Issue Data Breach Alerts, Say Critical Information on Several Customers Compromised
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America and TD Bank are disclosing data breaches that are placing some customers’ accounts and personal information at risk. In new filings with the Massachusetts state government, Chase says at least four customers in the state are affected by multiple breaches.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/06/14/jpmorgan-chase-bank-of-america-and-td-bank-issue-data-breach-alerts-say-critical-information-on-several-customers-compromised/

data.title: Whales Dump Over 270 Million Cardano In One Week – Bearish Signal Or Shakeout?
data.description:
Cardano (ADA) is currently consolidating near a critical support zone that could shape the direction of its price action in the coming weeks. After a sharp 15% drop since Wednesday, ADA is showing signs of weakness as broader market sentiment sours amid rising geopolitical tensions. The conflict between Israel and Iran has injected significant volatility and uncertainty into global markets, spilling over into the crypto space. Related Reading: Solana Approaches Critical Support Amid Middle East Conflicts – Can Demand Hold? ADA’s recent losses reflect this risk-off environment, as investors become more cautious and liquidity thins
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/cardano/whales-dump-over-270-million-cardano-in-one-week-bearish-signal-or-shakeout/

data.title: ConsenSys founder: LINEA will be the first to launch tokens, and multiple protocols will issue tokens
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: In response to a user's question about whether MetaMask will issue tokens, Joseph Lubin, founder of ConsenSys, said that ConsenSys has developed short-term and medium-term strategic plans involving the protocolization of existing products and multiple new projects. Given that Web3 protocols usually rely on tokens to operate, multiple protocols will launch tokens in the future. Lubin revealed that the first token to be issued will be LINEA, and subsequent protocols will also achieve coordinated development.

data.title: France eyes Bitcoin mining as energy management solution
data.description:
